plugins.name: CoreTools
plugins.role: Configuration-driven RPG automation host with scripting, variables, stations, shops, auction, economy, profile data, and multi-provider items.
plugins.architecture: A broad Bukkit composition root creates feature managers, then DelayedHooks activates optional integrations. Configured event names produce mutable Context objects that flow through condition, targeter, mechanic, and ScriptQueue factories.
api_boundaries: CoreTools singleton and manager getters are semi-public, not a separately versioned API
Context, Condition, Mechanic, targeters, and ScriptQueue form the scripting surface
MultiSourceItemCreator.add registers external item resolvers
PlayerDataLoadedEvent, VariableEvent, StationConfirmEvent, and StationItemEvent expose transitions
risks: Semi-public singleton access creates reload and order coupling
Decompiled save paths contain suspicious UUID reference comparisons and need runtime confirmation
Script discovery assumes safe directory traversal and should not be copied without stronger guards

plugins.name: MMOCore
plugins.role: Character progression platform for classes, skills, attributes, professions, quests, social groups, resources, loot, and waypoints.
plugins.architecture: onLoad registers definitions before onEnable resolves data and integrations. PlayerData is the aggregate root; stable-ID registries and MMOLoader factories turn configuration into extensible domain objects.
api_boundaries: MMOCoreAPI for player, party, and skill operations
MMOCoreRegister and MMOLoadManager for registered objects and config loaders
PlayerData and ExperienceObject for progression state
PartyModule, GuildModule, and QuestModule for external system adapters
Class, XP, resource, skill, loot, and item-unlock events
risks: Some formula behavior inferred from decompilation appears to use current rather than requested level
Mutable singleton managers should be wrapped behind addon-owned adapters
Bukkit mutations must stay on the correct scheduler

plugins.name: MMOInventory
plugins.role: Custom equipment inventory layouts, slot restrictions, player inventory data, and equip/use/death interaction.
plugins.architecture: InventoryManager owns configured layouts, RestrictionManager parses slot rules, and provider-backed player data switches between compact serialized and live inventory representations. Events bridge changes into MMOItems equipment resolution.
api_boundaries: InventoryManager and inventory/slot models
RestrictionManager registration before enable/freeze
InventoryProvider and PlayerData storage access
InventoryUpdateEvent, ItemEquipEvent, and PlayerInteractCustomSlotsEvent
Legacy RPGInventory compatibility shim
risks: The nominal MMOInventoryAPI wrapper is empty; effective contracts are managers, models, and events
Removed or renumbered slots need migration before old serialized data is loaded
Slot and item restrictions exist at separate layers and must both be enforced

plugins.name: MMOItems
plugins.role: Data-driven item platform for item types, templates, stats, randomized generation, crafting, upgrades, gems, sets, tiers, drops, and custom blocks.
plugins.architecture: Type plus template configuration becomes an MMOItemTemplate and MMOItemBuilder, then stat data, modifiers, tier, and level are applied before ItemStackBuilder emits NBT, lore, and events. Equipment updates are buffered before derived effects are recomputed.
api_boundaries: StatManager and ItemStat contracts
TypeManager, TemplateManager, MMOItemTemplate, and MMOItemBuilder
CraftingManager ingredient/output/condition/trigger registration
PlayerInventoryManager and InventorySupplier
Build, lore, crafting, equip, gem, repair, reforge, upgrade, and durability events
risks: Per-stat build failures may yield partially built items, which is unsafe for economy-critical flows
Effective tier fallback and the builder's exposed tier may differ in this snapshot
Custom stats and types must register in the correct lifecycle window

plugins.name: MMOProfiles
plugins.role: Multiple character profiles with vanilla state snapshots, plugin-owned data modules, placeholders, and proxy handoff.
plugins.architecture: ProfileProvider is registered through Bukkit Services. A pending profile transition waits for registered ProfileDataModule participants, validates the snapshot, applies state, and emits selected/unload events. Velocity and Bukkit exchange a profile request envelope.
api_boundaries: ProfileProvider Bukkit service
ProfileDataModule and pending lifecycle events
PlayerProfile and ProfileList models
PlaceholderProcessor
Create, remove, pending, select, autosave, unload, and proxy-selection events
risks: A module that never validates can stall a profile transition; addon modules should complete in finally and add diagnostics
Create-event cancellation behavior is suspicious in this decompile and requires runtime verification
Profile identity and addon state must be keyed by stable UUID/profile IDs, not display text

plugins.name: ModelEngine
plugins.role: Packet/NMS-backed entity models, bones, animation, VFX, hitboxes, mounts, screens, model import, visibility, culling, and persistence callbacks.
plugins.architecture: ModelEngineAPI is installed during load. BaseEntity, ModeledEntity, ActiveModel, and bone behavior managers form the model aggregate. Registries supply blueprints, behaviors, animation handlers, keyframes, scripts, mounts, and pivots while DualTicker coordinates phased work.
api_boundaries: ModelEngineAPI facade
ModeledEntity, ActiveModel, ModelBlueprint, ModelBone, and behavior contracts
AnimationHandler and state/priority handler registries
RegisterBehaviorParserEvent and model lifecycle events
PlatformScheduler and NMSHandler, with NMS treated as volatile
risks: Static facade calls are lifecycle-sensitive
Late behavior registration can miss cached serialization setup
NMS/network APIs and cross-thread model state are high-risk integration boundaries

plugins.name: MythicCrucible
plugins.role: Mythic item traits, furniture, custom blocks, armor, equippables, recipes, augments, sets, world generation, and resource-pack generation/deployment.
plugins.architecture: LuminePlugin lifecycle binds reloadable managers and Mythic components. ItemManager wraps Mythic items with a recursion guard, performs second-pass resolution, and dispatches generation events. PackGenerationManager composes generated and external assets before deployment.
api_boundaries: MythicCrucibleProvider and furniture-focused MythicCrucibleAPI
ItemManager and feature submanagers are semi-public
Craft, pack, and furniture lifecycle events
Mythic mechanics, conditions, targeters, and placeholders
risks: The explicit API is narrow; manager getters are more version-sensitive
Writable provider state may remain stale across unsupported hot reloads
Pack generation and Bukkit world operations need scheduler discipline

plugins.name: MythicDungeons
plugins.role: Classic, continuous, and procedural dungeon instances with rooms, queues, parties, conditions, triggers, functions, rewards, spectators, and moving blocks.
plugins.architecture: A Bukkit composition root registers dungeon/layout types and graph elements, validates queue requirements, creates isolated instances, evaluates trigger-condition-function graphs, emits lifecycle events, restores players, and disposes generated worlds. Procedural Layout implementations place weighted room connectors under bounds constraints.
api_boundaries: MythicDungeonsService from Bukkit ServicesManager
DungeonFunction, DungeonTrigger, and TriggerCondition extension bases
Dungeon/layout/type and instance-listener registration
Dungeon, room, loot, trigger, player, and party events
Semi-public AbstractDungeon, AbstractInstance, Layout, and manager objects
risks: Two service booleans appear to report false after successful actions in decompiled flow
IDungeonParty.hasPlayer appears to use UUID reference equality
Mutable manager views and recursive generated-world deletion demand strict path and lifecycle isolation

plugins.name: MythicEnchants
plugins.role: Data-driven native/custom enchant definitions, datapack output, custom enchanting, reagents, anvil/grindstone behavior, trigger skills, and runtime addons.
plugins.architecture: YAML definitions populate enchant and tag registries plus native datapack output. Runtime listeners resolve equipment and event triggers, fire cancellable Mench events, then dispatch Mythic skills. AddonManager loads isolated addon jars through AddonContext.
api_boundaries: MythicEnchantsAPI for query, apply, remove, register, tags, offers, and validity
EnchantmentDefinition builder and EnchantmentRegistry
MythicEnchantsAddon and AddonContext
Apply, remove, prepare-offer, and skill-trigger events
Mythic mechanics, conditions, targeting helpers, and 39 trigger families
risks: Late API registration alone does not add the enchant to Bukkit's native registry
Addon api-version is parsed but lifecycle registrations may not be fully tracked for reload cleanup
Configured storage options and actual YAML stores need runtime validation

plugins.name: MythicRPG
plugins.role: Profiles, archetypes, talents, points, experience, spells, resources, casting UI, death rules, waystones, parties, friends, shared rewards, and economy.
plugins.architecture: LuminePlugin lifecycle loads config-driven domain definitions and binds managers. MythicRPGAPI exposes immutable DTO views and opens live ProfileSession wrappers. JSON or SQL repositories persist profile state with versioned SQL migrations.
api_boundaries: MythicRPGProvider and the 92-operation MythicRPGAPI
ProfileSession for player-bound operations
Immutable archetype, profile, party, point, spell, friend, and waystone DTOs
Progression, spell, talent, profile, reagent, and archetype events
Mythic mechanics, conditions, targeters, and manager registries
risks: The explicit API operates on loaded/live profiles and is not a general offline mutation API
Two loaded events expose cancellation methods without implementing Bukkit Cancellable
Snapshot version and broad aliases require exact-artifact consumer tests
